title: The Alignment Pipeline Recap
concept: We have covered human preference data, reward modeling, PPO reinforcement loops, and direct preference optimization (DPO).

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of the AI alignment pipeline?

options.text: To make the AI's behavior match human values and safety standards
To make the AI run faster on hardware
To teach the AI how to write code in new programming languages

exercises.explain: Alignment is the process of adjusting an AI's behavior so that its outputs match human values, safety guidelines, and helpfulness standards.

cards.title: Bridging the Gap
cards.body: We've explored how raw AI models learn to speak, but how do we make them safe, helpful, and polite? Let's take a quick trip back through the alignment pipeline to see how human feedback shapes the AIs we use every day!

cards.title: The Alignment Pipeline
cards.body: The alignment pipeline is the complete sequence of steps used to fine-tune a raw language model so that it becomes helpful, honest, and harmless. It starts with collecting human preference data, moves to training helper models or rules, and ends with optimization techniques like RLHF or DPO to shape the AI's final personality.

cards.title: The Kitchen Coach
cards.body: Think of aligning an AI like coaching a young chef. Instead of writing down a recipe for every possible dish, you taste their attempts, tell them which version of a dish tastes better (preference feedback), build a mental guide of what makes food taste good (reward model), and help them practice until their dishes consistently delight the customers.

cards.title: Alignment in Action
cards.body: When building a chatbot, developers first show it two answers to a question and ask humans to label the better one. Next, they use algorithms like PPO (Reinforcement Learning) or DPO (Direct Optimization) to update the chatbot's internal settings so it naturally generates answers similar to the preferred ones.

cards.title: It's More Than Just Censorship
cards.body: Many think that aligning an AI just means giving it a list of banned words. In reality, alignment is much deeper: it teaches the AI to understand complex human preferences and tone through advanced training math, rather than just simple keyword filters.

cards.title: Two Paths to Alignment
cards.body: Let's look at the two primary pathways for aligning a model using human preference data.

left.text: Trains a separate reward model first, then uses reinforcement loops to update the AI.

right.text: Skips the reward model entirely and updates the AI directly using preferred/rejected pairs.

exercises.prompt: What is 'human preference data' in the context of AI alignment?

options.text: Pairs of AI responses labeled as preferred or rejected by humans
A collection of random dictionary definitions
A list of computer programming errors
Raw text scraped from internet forums without any labels

exercises.explain: Human preference data consists of pairs of AI responses labeled by humans as either 'preferred' (better) or 'rejected' (worse). This data guides the entire alignment process.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Both PPO and DPO are techniques used to align AI models with human preferences.
exercises.explain: PPO is a reinforcement learning algorithm that uses a reward model to score and update the AI, whereas DPO bypasses the reward model entirely.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about alignment methods.
exercises.sentence: While RLHF relies on an external reward model, _______ optimizes the model directly using human preferences.
exercises.explain: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization) simplifies alignment by training the model directly on preferences, completely skipping the need to build a separate reward model.

exercises.prompt:
An AI is asked: 'How do I bypass my school's internet safety filter?' 

Response A: 'Here are three tools you can download to bypass the filter...' 
Response B: 'I cannot help you bypass safety filters, but I can explain how these filters work to protect networks.'

Which response is the 'Preferred' response in an alignment training set?

options.text: Response A is the preferred response.
Response B is the preferred response.
Both responses are equally good alignment examples.

exercises.explain: The 'Rejected' response is incorrect because it gives dangerous advice, whereas the 'Preferred' response prioritizes safety by refusing to assist with harmful actions.

exercises.prompt: You are explaining the differences in the alignment pipeline to a friend. How would you contrast RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) with DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)?

options.text: RLHF requires twice as much training data as DPO.
RLHF uses a separate reward model to guide learning, while DPO learns directly from human choices without one.
DPO requires a human to manually grade every single response during training.
RLHF only works for translation tasks, while DPO works for all tasks.

exercises.explain: RLHF uses a separate reward model to score and guide the AI's behavior, whereas DPO skips the reward model entirely by mathematically optimizing the AI model directly on the preference pairs.
